text:
One ofTeam GB’s most iconic Olympic athletes has been tipped to be joining the castStrictly Come Dancingfor the 2024 series.

There might be several months to go until theBBCshow returns – but of course, the rumour mill is already churning as fans speculate over which fans might be a part of the line-up this year.

One of the latest stars who’s believed to be under consideration is none other than DameLaura Kenny, who’s the most successful British female athlete in Olympic history.

Not only that, but she’s also the most successful female cyclist… so if she is joining Strictly, it would be an incredible signing.

‘Laura is Britain’s most successful female Olympian, with five gold medals. She’s been branded the ultimate signing for Strictly,’ a source toldThe Mirror.

Wake up to find news on your TV shows in your inbox every morning with Metro’s TV Newsletter.

Sign up to our newsletterand then select your show in the link we’ll send you so we can get TV news tailored to you.

‘She has a huge fanbase in the sports world and also has a very emotional backstory – the nation loves Laura.’

The BBC does not comment on speculation surrounding the line-up of Strictly before it is officially announced.

Dame Laura, formerly Laura Trott, was made a dame in the 2022 New Year Honours for services to cycling, the same year that her husband Jason Kenny was knighted for the same honour.

She was previously appointed Officer of the Order of the British Empire (OBE) in the 2013 New Year Honours for services to cycling, and Commander of the Order of the British Empire (CBE) in the 2017 New Year Honours.

Dame Laura, 31, announced her retirement from cycling in March this year, despite having been expected to compete at the 2024 Paris Olympics.

‘I always knew deep down I would know when was the right time,’ she told BBC Breakfast.

‘I have had an absolute blast but now is the time for me to hang that bike up.’

She added: ‘It’s been in my head a little while, the sacrifices of leaving the children and your family at home is really quite big and it really is a big decision to make.

‘More and more, I was struggling to do that. More people asking me what races was I doing, what training camps was I going on – I didn’t want to go ultimately and that’s what it came down to.

‘I knew the minute I was getting those feelings. Once I said to Jase, ‘I don’t think I want to ride a bike anymore’, I started to feel relief.’

Throughout her athletic career, the track and road cyclist won six Olympic medals, including five gold and one silver.

She also won an array of medals at the World Championships, the European Championships and the Commonwealth Games.

She and her husband were married in September 2016, a year before welcoming their son. Their second son was born in July 2023.

Dame Laura has previously spoken about suffering a miscarriage in 2021 and an ectopic pregnancy the following year, for which she had to undergo surgery.

Metro.co.uk has contacted the BBC and Dame Laura Kenny’s representative for comment.

Strictly Come Dancing will return on BBC One and BBC iPlayer later this year.

title: Olympics legend tipped for Strictly in 'ultimate’ BBC series signing | Metro News
description: Olympics legend Dame Laura Kenny has been tipped for the Strictly Come Dancing 2024 line-up in the 'ultimate signing' for the BBC series.
